---
title: "Title of Section 1"
---

<!-- You have to develop your own headline structure. -->

## Data Preprocessing
### Data importing
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import zipfile # For handling zip files

# Upload zip.folder from ESS website 
zip_path = "ESS9e03_3-ESS10e03_3-ESS10SCe03_2-ESS11e04_1-subset.zip"
csv_inside = "ESS9e03_3-ESS10e03_3-ESS10SCe03_2-ESS11e04_1-subset.csv"

with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, 'r') as z:
        with z.open(csv_inside) as f:
            df = pd.read_csv(f, low_memory=False)

print(" CSV Load Successful!")
print(f"Dataset Size: {df.shape[0]} rows x {df.shape[1]} columns")
```

### Data Exploration
```{python}

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Dataset Dimensions (Rows, Columns)
print(f"### 1. Dataset Dimensions")
print(f"The dataset contains {df.shape[0]} respondents and {df.shape[1]} variables.")
# Data Structure
print("\n### 2. Data Structure")
print("Column Names (First 50):")
print(df.columns.tolist()[:50])

# 3. Raw Data Preview
print("\n### 3. Raw Data Preview (Selected Columns)")
# pick only the columns of interest to display: country, birth year, and some human values questions
interest_cols = ['cntry', 'yrbrn'] + [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith('ip')][:5]
print(df[interest_cols].head())

# Statistical Summary
print("\n### 4. Statistical Summary of Values & Birth Year")
key_vars = ['yrbrn'] + [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith('ip')][:10]
display(df[key_vars].describe().T)

# Diversity
print("\n### 5. Country Distribution (Top 10)")
display(df['cntry'].value_counts().head(10))

```



### Data Cleansing
# --- 1. Handling Missing Data (Refusals, Don't Knows) ---
# We replace ESS-specific missing codes with NaN (Not a Number)
value_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith('ip')]
df[value_cols] = df[value_cols].replace([7, 8, 9], np.nan)
df['lrscale'] = df['lrscale'].replace([77, 88, 99], np.nan)
df['yrbrn'] = df['yrbrn'].replace([9999], np.nan)

# --- 2. Reverse Coding the Values ---
# Transformation: (Max + 1) - original_value
# This ensures 6 = "Very much like me" and 1 = "Not at all like me"
df[value_cols] = 7 - df[value_cols]

# --- 3. Creating Generational Cohorts ---
conditions = [
    (df['yrbrn'] >= 1997),
    (df['yrbrn'] >= 1981) & (df['yrbrn'] <= 1996),
    (df['yrbrn'] >= 1965) & (df['yrbrn'] <= 1980),
    (df['yrbrn'] <= 1964)
]
labels = ['Gen Z', 'Millennials', 'Gen X', 'Boomers']
df['generation'] = np.select(conditions, labels, default='Unknown')

# --- 4. Creating Schwartz Value Dimensions (The Full 10) ---
# We calculate the mean of specific items to create the core value pillars
df['Universalism'] = df[['ipudrst', 'iphlppl', 'ipenvir']].mean(axis=1)
df['Benevolence'] = df[['iphlppl', 'ipudrst']].mean(axis=1)
df['Tradition'] = df[['imptrad', 'ipmodst']].mean(axis=1)
df['Security'] = df[['impsafe', 'ipstrgv']].mean(axis=1)
df['Conformity'] = df[['ipfrule', 'ipbhvar']].mean(axis=1)
df['Self-Direction'] = df[['impfree', 'ipgdtim']].mean(axis=1)
df['Stimulation'] = df[['ipstmlt', 'iphlth']].mean(axis=1)
df['Hedonism'] = df[['ipgdtim', 'impdiff']].mean(axis=1)
df['Achievement'] = df[['ipeqopt', 'ipshlpp']].mean(axis=1)
df['Power'] = df[['iprich', 'iprspot']].mean(axis=1)

# List of Master Value columns for easy access in Section 2
schwartz_values = [
    'Universalism', 'Benevolence', 'Tradition', 'Security', 'Conformity',
    'Self-Direction', 'Stimulation', 'Hedonism', 'Achievement', 'Power'
]

print("Data Cleansing Complete: Missing values handled and generations assigned.")

# --- 5. Cleaned Data Preview ---
# Verification check for the project team
print("\n### Cleaned Data Preview (Transformed Variables)")
display(df[['generation', 'lrscale'] + schwartz_values].head())

```

## Headline 2
